metadata.seed: I mean, I'm a photographer, so I kind of. It's been stopping me a bit, my anxiety from being able to get work, and because every time there's an opportunity that is kind of involving me having to interact with people, I just tend to just turn it down. So I was kind of making things hard in that respect and just kind of friends wise and everything, I feel like I'm just kind of increasingly more alone. So it's kind of stopping me from making friends and, you know, like, relationships and stuff.

patient.query: I mean, I'm a photographer, so I kind of. So it's kind of stopping me from making friends and, you know, like, relationships and stuff.

patient.query: I think it’s mostly when I’m in social settings where I don’t know a lot of people... like parties or networking events. Just the pressure of making a good impression is so intense. And sometimes even just casual chats, like at work, can feel overwhelming. It’s like I’m constantly on edge, waiting for something to go wrong.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does get to me. I mean, I want to connect with people and be part of things, but then that pressure just takes over. I start imagining all these scenarios where I mess up or get rejected. It feels like I’m just stuck in this loop of worrying, and it’s hard to break out of it.

automatic_thoughts: I'm stuck in a loop of worrying

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: It’s just... when I think about those situations, I often remember times when I’ve stumbled over my words or felt ignored. It’s like those moments just replay in my head, and I convince myself that it’ll happen again. Even if it’s not always true, that fear feels really real in the moment. I guess I just end up expecting the worst, which makes me hesitant to even try.

automatic_thoughts: I’ll stumble over my words again

It’s like I hold onto those memories and think, “What if that happens again?” I mean, I know not every situation will be like that, but it’s hard to shake off those fears. I wish I could just let go of those past moments and feel more confident moving forward.
